symptoms.symptom_name: Nappy-area rash beginning 1-2 days after a diarrhoeal illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Faecal proteases/lipases are markedly increased during and after diarrhoea, driving irritant breakdown of the nappy-area skin.

symptoms.symptom_name: Erythema over convex surfaces with sparing of the deep skin folds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Convex-surface involvement with fold-sparing is the defining distribution of irritant napkin dermatitis.
Candida characteristically involves the deep folds; sparing of the folds argues strongly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of satellite pustules beyond the rash border

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Satellite pustules are a hallmark of candidal dermatitis; their absence helps exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bright red, shiny, glazed and chapped skin surface

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A glazed, chapped erythematous surface reflects irritant barrier breakdown of the stratum corneum.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain and distress on voiding and on contact with urine/faeces

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Contact of urine/faeces with eroded, irritant-damaged skin is characteristically painful.

symptoms.symptom_name: No honey-coloured crust, pus, bullae or ulceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Honey crust, pus and bullae signal staphylococcal/streptococcal infection; their absence argues against current secondary infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with moist mucosa, alert, capillary refill under 2 seconds (clinician-measured infant observations)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and systemic compromise argues against systemic infection; these measured infant vitals are clinician-obtained and sealed, the AI Doctor infers wellness from carer-reported alertness and feeding instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced feeding for 12 hours with preserved urine output and moist mucosa

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain-related feeding refusal after diarrhoea is a dehydration risk; currently compensated but a safety-net trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: KOH microscopy / skin swab to confirm or exclude candidal or bacterial infection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: KOH microscopy would definitively confirm/exclude candida in atypical cases, but is not indicated here and is unavailable in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Post-diarrhoeal onset plus convex-surface, fold-sparing glazed erythema without satellite lesions is diagnostic of irritant napkin dermatitis and excludes the candidal mimic.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a classic irritant pattern with fold-sparing and no satellite lesions; reserved for atypical or treatment-refractory cases, and unavailable via telehealth.
No honey crust, pus or systemic features to suggest secondary bacterial infection; would only be considered if the safety-net triggers develop, at an in-person review.
